title: RRB Section Controller (Indian Railways) - 200 Expected Practice Questions
note: Covers the three CBT sections: Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, and General Awareness. Practice set for preparation purposes; verify current-affairs items closer to your exam date.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 12, 18, and 24?
What is the HCF of 84 and 126?
Find the smallest number which when divided by 6, 8, and 10 leaves remainder 2 in each case.
questions.explanation: LCM(6,8,10)=120. Required number = 120+2 = 122.

questions.question: What is the value of 15% of 240?
If 3x + 7 = 22, what is the value of x?
What is the sum of the first 20 natural numbers?
A number when divided by 342 gives a remainder 47. What is the remainder when the same number is divided by 18?
questions.explanation: 342 = 18*19, so 342 is a multiple of 18. Remainder 47 divided by 18 gives remainder 11 (47=18*2+11).

questions.question: Which of the following is a prime number?
questions.explanation: 97 is not divisible by any number other than 1 and itself, so it is prime; 91=7*13, 87=3*29, 77=7*11.

questions.question: What is the square root of 1764?
Find the value of (0.25)^2.
The product of two consecutive positive integers is 342. What is the smaller integer?
questions.explanation: 18*19 = 342, so the smaller of the two consecutive integers is 18.

questions.question: What is the remainder when 2^50 is divided by 7?
questions.explanation: 2^3=8 is congruent to 1 (mod 7), so 2^50=2^(48+2)=(2^3)^16 * 2^2  is congruent to  1^16*4 = 4 (mod 7).

questions.question: Which of the following numbers is divisible by 11?
questions.explanation: For 70169: alternating sum = (7+1+9)-(0+6)=17-6=11, divisible by 11.

questions.question: Find the value of: 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/6
A student scored 462 marks out of 700. What percentage of marks did the student score?
If the price of an item is increased by 25%, by what percentage should the new price be decreased to bring it back to the original price?
A shopkeeper buys an article for Rs. 800 and sells it for Rs. 920. What is his profit percentage?
A man sells an article at a loss of 10%. If he had sold it for Rs. 60 more, he would have gained 5%. What is the cost price of the article?
Find the simple interest on Rs. 5000 for 3 years at 8% per annum.
What is the compound interest on Rs. 2000 for 2 years at 10% per annum, compounded annually?
Divide Rs. 1200 between A and B in the ratio 3:5. What is B's share?
questions.explanation: Total parts = 8. B's share = (5/8)*1200 = 750.

questions.question: The ratio of ages of A and B is 3:4. If the sum of their ages is 35 years, what is A's age?
The average of 5 numbers is 27. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 25. What is the excluded number?
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 135. Sum of 4 numbers = 100. Excluded number = 135-100 = 35.

questions.question: The average weight of 10 students is 42 kg. If a new student weighing 53 kg joins, what is the new average weight?
questions.explanation: Total weight = 420+53=473. New average = 473/11 = 43 kg.

questions.question: A mixture of 40 litres contains milk and water in the ratio 3:1. How much water should be added to make the ratio 3:2?
questions.explanation: Milk=30L, Water=10L. For ratio 3:2, water needed = (30*2)/3 = 20L, so add 20-10=10L.

questions.question: If A can do a piece of work in 12 days and B can do it in 15 days, in how many days will they complete it together?
A train 150 m long crosses a pole in 15 seconds. What is its speed in km/h?
A car covers a distance of 240 km in 4 hours. What is its speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance/Time = 240/4 = 60 km/h.

questions.question: Two trains 120 m and 180 m long run in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 36 km/h respectively. In how much time will they cross each other?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 54+36=90 km/h = 25 m/s. Total length = 300m. Time = 300/25 = 12 seconds.

questions.question: What is the area of a rectangle with length 15 cm and breadth 8 cm?
questions.explanation: Area = length * breadth = 15*8 = 120 sq cm.

questions.question: What is the perimeter of a square with side 9 cm?
questions.explanation: Perimeter of square = 4*side = 4*9 = 36 cm.

questions.question: What is the area of a circle with radius 7 cm? (Use pi = 22/7)
What is the volume of a cube with side 5 cm?
questions.explanation: Volume of cube = side^3 = 5^3 = 125 cubic cm.

questions.question: What is the total surface area of a cube with edge 6 cm?
questions.explanation: Total surface area of cube = 6*side^2 = 6*36 = 216 sq cm.

questions.question: The volume of a cylinder with radius 7 cm and height 10 cm is (use pi=22/7):
questions.explanation: Volume = pi*r^2*h = (22/7)*49*10 = 1540 cubic cm.

questions.question: The area of a triangle with base 12 cm and height 8 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area of triangle = (1/2)*base*height = (1/2)*12*8 = 48 sq cm.

questions.question: If x + y = 10 and x - y = 4, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Adding both equations: 2x=14, x=7.

questions.question: If x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0, what are the roots of the equation?
What is the value of a^2 - b^2 if a+b=12 and a-b=4?
A sum of Rs. 6000 becomes Rs. 7200 in 2 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?
The ages of A and B are in the ratio 5:7. After 4 years, the ratio will be 3:4. What is A's present age?
questions.explanation: Let ages be 5x and 7x. (5x+4)/(7x+4)=3/4. 20x+16=21x+12, x=4. A's age = 5*4=20 years.

questions.question: In a class of 60 students, 40% are girls. How many boys are there in the class?
A bag contains coins of Re. 1, 50-paise, and 25-paise in the ratio 3:4:12, with a total value of Rs. 600. How many 25-paise coins are there?
The following table shows marks scored by a student in 5 subjects: Maths-85, Science-78, English-72, Hindi-80, Social Science-75. What is the average marks scored?
A pie chart shows a company's expenses: Salaries 40%, Rent 20%, Marketing 15%, Utilities 10%, Others 15%. If total expense is Rs. 10,00,000, what is the amount spent on Marketing?
If the numerator of a fraction is increased by 20% and the denominator is decreased by 10%, the fraction becomes 4/5. What was the original fraction?
The compound interest on a sum for 2 years at 5% per annum is Rs. 41. What is the simple interest on the same sum for the same time and rate?
questions.explanation: This is a classic CI-SI relation problem where SI is slightly less than CI; with these standard values SI = Rs. 40.

questions.question: A can complete a work in 20 days and B in 30 days. They work together for 5 days, after which A leaves. In how many more days will B finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Combined rate for 5 days = 5*(1/20+1/30) = 5*(5/60) = 25/60 = 5/12 of work done. Remaining = 7/12. B alone finishes remaining in (7/12)*30 = 17.5 days.

questions.question: What is the value of 7! (7 factorial)?
In how many ways can the letters of the word 'RAIL' be arranged?
questions.explanation: 'RAIL' has 4 distinct letters, so arrangements = 4! = 24.

questions.question: A boat goes 30 km downstream in 2 hours and returns upstream in 3 hours. What is the speed of the boat in still water?
questions.explanation: Downstream speed=15 km/h, Upstream speed=10 km/h. Speed in still water = (15+10)/2 = 12.5 km/h.

questions.question: The probability of getting a head when a fair coin is tossed once is:
questions.explanation: A fair coin has two equally likely outcomes, so the probability of heads is 1/2.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting a sum of 7 when two dice are rolled?
questions.explanation: There are 6 favourable outcomes (1,6),(2,5),(3,4),(4,3),(5,2),(6,1) out of 36 total, giving 6/36=1/6.

questions.question: If the perimeter of a rectangle is 60 cm and its length is 18 cm, what is its area?
questions.explanation: Perimeter=2(l+b)=60, so l+b=30, b=30-18=12. Area=l*b=18*12=216 sq cm.

questions.question: What is the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are 4,6,8,10,12 - each term is n(n+1): 42=6*7.

questions.question: A sum of money doubles itself in 8 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become 4 times itself?
questions.explanation: If P doubles in 8 years, SI per 8 years = P, so rate implies triple growth (from P to 4P is 3P interest) takes 24 years.

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods 20% above cost price and allows a discount of 10%. What is his profit percentage?
What is the value of (2/3) of 90 minus (1/5) of 50?
A train running at 72 km/h crosses a platform 250 m long in 26 seconds. What is the length of the train?
questions.explanation: Speed = 72 km/h = 20 m/s. Distance covered = 20*26 = 520 m. Length of train = 520-250 = 270 m.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series: 3, 6, 11, 18, 27, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are 3,5,7,9,11 (odd numbers). 27+11=38.

questions.question: Find the missing term: 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, ?
questions.explanation: These are squares of 2,3,4,5,6,7: 7^2=49.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: 8, 27, 64, 125, 216, 300
questions.explanation: 8,27,64,125,216 are perfect cubes (2^3 to 6^3); 300 is not a perfect cube.

questions.question: Find the next letter in the series: B, D, F, H, ?
questions.explanation: The series skips one letter each time: B,D,F,H,J.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Book is to Library as Painting is to ?
questions.explanation: A library houses books, similarly a gallery houses paintings.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Doctor is to Hospital as Teacher is to ?
questions.explanation: A doctor works in a hospital; a teacher works in a school.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Foot is to Leg as Hand is to ?
questions.explanation: Foot is part of the leg; hand is part of the arm.

questions.question: In a certain code, 'CAT' is written as 'DBU'. How is 'DOG' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one: D->E, O->P, G->H, giving 'EPH'.

questions.question: In a certain code, 'FLOWER' is written as 'EKNVDQ'. How is 'GARDEN' written in that code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted backward by one: F,Z,Q,C,D,M -> 'FZQCDM'.

questions.question: If 'PAPER' is coded as '45274', 'PEN' is coded as '456' and 'REAP' is coded as '7245', what is the code for 'A'?
questions.explanation: Comparing the codes, letter A consistently corresponds to digit 2.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Potato, Banana
questions.explanation: Apple, Mango, and Banana are fruits, while Potato is a vegetable.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: Triangle, Square, Circle, Cube
questions.explanation: Triangle, Square, and Circle are 2D shapes, while a Cube is a 3D shape.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: 121, 144, 169, 200, 225
Pointing to a photograph, a man says, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: The man's grandfather's only son is the man's father, so the woman is the man's sister.

questions.question: A is the brother of B. B is the sister of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: Since C is D's father and A is C's sibling, A is D's uncle (or aunt).

questions.question: Introducing a boy, a girl said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the girl related to the boy?
questions.explanation: The only daughter of the girl's mother is the girl herself, so the girl is the boy's mother.

questions.question: A man walks 5 km towards South, then turns left and walks 3 km. He then turns left again and walks 5 km. How far is he from his starting point?
questions.explanation: Tracing the path shows he ends up 3 km to the east of the starting point (the two 5 km legs cancel out, leaving the 3 km sideways movement).

questions.question: Ravi walks 10 m towards North, then turns right and walks 10 m, then turns right again and walks 10 m. In which direction is he now from his starting point?
questions.explanation: After tracing the path (North, then East, then South), he ends up 10 m to the east of the starting point.

questions.question: If South-East becomes North, North-East becomes West, and so on, what will North become?
questions.explanation: Each direction is rotated 135 degrees clockwise in this scheme; North becomes South-East.

questions.question: In a row of 40 students, Raj is 12th from the left end. What is his position from the right end?
questions.explanation: Position from right = (total - position from left) + 1 = (40-12)+1 = 29.

questions.question: In a class, Meena's rank is 15th from the top and 22nd from the bottom. How many students are there in the class?
questions.explanation: Total students = (rank from top + rank from bottom) - 1 = 15+22-1 = 36.

questions.question: Five friends A, B, C, D, and E are sitting in a row. B is to the right of A, and C is to the left of A. D is to the right of B, and E is to the left of C. Who is sitting at the extreme left?
questions.explanation: The order from left to right is E, C, A, B, D, so E is at the extreme left.

questions.question: What was the day of the week on 15th August 1947?
questions.explanation: 15th August 1947 was a Friday.

questions.question: If today is Wednesday, what will be the day after 65 days?
questions.explanation: 65 days = 9 weeks + 2 days. Since 65 mod 7 = 2, Wednesday + 2 days = Friday.

questions.question: At what time between 3 and 4 o'clock will the hands of a clock be together?
questions.explanation: Using the standard formula, the hands coincide at approximately 3:16:22.

questions.question: How many times do the hands of a clock coincide in a day?
questions.explanation: The hands of a clock coincide 22 times in a 24-hour day.

questions.question: What is the angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 4:00?
questions.explanation: At 4:00, the angle between hands = 4*30 = 120 degrees.

questions.question: If 2nd January 2005 was a Sunday, what day of the week was 2nd January 2006?
questions.explanation: 2005 is not a leap year, so it has 365 days = 52 weeks + 1 day. The day advances by 1, giving Monday.

questions.question: Statements: All cats are animals. All animals are living beings. Conclusion I: All cats are living beings. Conclusion II: All living beings are cats. Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Since all cats are animals and all animals are living beings, all cats are living beings (Conclusion I). Conclusion II is not necessarily true.

questions.question: Statements: Some pens are books. Some books are tables. Conclusion I: Some pens are tables. Conclusion II: Some tables are pens. Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Since the middle term 'books' is not fully distributed between pens and tables, no definite conclusion about pens and tables can be drawn.

questions.question: In a group of people, 30 like tea, 25 like coffee, and 10 like both. How many people like at least one of the two drinks?
questions.explanation: Using the formula n(A union B) = n(A)+n(B)-n(A intersect B) = 30+25-10 = 45.

questions.question: In a survey, 60 people like cricket, 40 like football, and 20 like both. How many like only cricket?
questions.explanation: Only cricket = Total cricket - Both = 60-20 = 40.

questions.question: Choose the word which is least like the other words: Cow, Goat, Sheep, Tiger, Buffalo
questions.explanation: Cow, Goat, Sheep, and Buffalo are domestic/herbivorous farm animals, while Tiger is a wild carnivore.

questions.question: Choose the odd pair out: (Delhi, India), (London, England), (Paris, Germany), (Tokyo, Japan)
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital of France, not Germany, making this pair incorrect.

questions.question: If '+' means '/', '-' means 'x', 'x' means '-', and '/' means '+', find the value of: 20 - 4 + 2 x 3 / 5
questions.explanation: Substituting the meanings gives: 20 x 4 / 2 - 3 + 5. Following order of operations: 20x4=80, 80/2=40, then 40-3+5=42.

questions.question: If A means 'added to', B means 'subtracted from', C means 'multiplied by', and D means 'divided by', what is the value of 10 A 6 C 2 B 8 D 4?
questions.explanation: 10 + 6*2 - 8/4 = 10 + 12 - 2 = 20, following standard operator precedence (multiplication and division before addition and subtraction).

questions.question: Which figure will complete the pattern when the mirror is placed along the given line? (Conceptual: mirror image reasoning)
questions.explanation: A mirror image reverses the figure left-to-right (or top-to-bottom depending on the mirror line), a standard rule in mirror-image reasoning questions.

questions.question: A cube is painted red on all its faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many smaller cubes have exactly one face painted?
questions.explanation: In a 3x3x3 cube cut, the smaller cubes with exactly one painted face are the centre cubes of each face, totaling 6.

questions.question: A cube is painted red on all its faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many smaller cubes have no face painted?
questions.explanation: In a 3x3x3 cube, only the single centre cube has no painted face.

questions.question: A cube is painted red on all its faces and then cut into 27 equal smaller cubes. How many smaller cubes have exactly two faces painted?
questions.explanation: The edge cubes (excluding corners) of a 3x3x3 cube have exactly two painted faces, totaling 12.

questions.question: How many corner (three-face painted) cubes are there when a cube is cut into 27 equal smaller cubes after painting all faces?
questions.explanation: A cube always has 8 corners, so 8 smaller cubes will have exactly three painted faces.

questions.question: Find the next term: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.explanation: These are perfect squares: 1^2,2^2,3^2,4^2,5^2,6^2=36.

questions.question: Find the missing number: 5, 10, 20, 40, ?
questions.explanation: Each term is double the previous term: 40*2=80.

questions.question: Find the next term: 100, 90, 81, 73, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are 10,9,8,7: 73-6=67.

questions.question: Find the odd one out: 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11
questions.explanation: 2,3,5,7,11 are prime numbers; 9 is not (9=3*3).

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Fish is to Water as Bird is to ?
questions.explanation: A fish lives/moves in water; a bird lives/moves in the sky.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Pen is to Write as Knife is to ?
questions.explanation: A pen is used to write; a knife is used to cut.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: 5 is to 25 as 7 is to ?
questions.explanation: The relationship is squaring: 5^2=25, so 7^2=49.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: Engine is to Train as Heart is to ?
questions.explanation: An engine powers/drives a train; a heart powers/drives the body.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'GO' is written as '715' and 'SO' is written as '1915'. How is 'SIT' written?
questions.explanation: Each letter is represented by its position in the alphabet: S=19, I=9, T=20, giving '19920'.

questions.question: If 'MADRAS' is coded as 'NBESBT', what is the code for 'BOMBAY'?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one: B->C, O->P, M->N, B->C, A->B, Y->Z, giving 'CPNCBZ'.

questions.question: P is the father of Q. Q is the sister of R. R is the son of S. How is S related to P?
questions.explanation: Since P is the father of Q and R, and S is R's parent, S must be P's spouse (wife).

questions.question: Looking at a portrait, Ramesh said, 'This man's son is my father's father, i.e., grandfather.' Whose portrait was Ramesh looking at?
questions.explanation: The man's son is Ramesh's grandfather, so the man himself is Ramesh's great-grandfather.

questions.question: Six friends P, Q, R, S, T, U are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Q is second to the right of P. R is immediate neighbour of Q and S. Who is sitting opposite to P if T is between U and S?
questions.explanation: Based on standard circular arrangement logic with the given clues, S ends up sitting directly opposite P.

questions.question: Arrange the words in a logical/dictionary order: 1. Cat 2. Cow 3. Camel 4. Crow
questions.explanation: Alphabetical order: Camel, Cat, Cow, Crow, which corresponds to sequence 3,1,2,4 i.e. option '1,3,2,4' in reversed labeling - the correct dictionary order is Camel, Cat, Cow, Crow.

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong with the others?
questions.explanation: Inch, Foot, and Yard are units of length, while Kilogram is a unit of mass.

questions.question: Find the missing number in the series: 7, 14, 28, 56, ?
questions.explanation: Each term is double the previous: 56*2=112.

questions.question: A is taller than B but shorter than C. D is taller than C but shorter than E. Who is the tallest?
questions.explanation: Order from shortest to tallest: B < A < C < D < E, so E is the tallest.

questions.question: In a certain code, 'TABLE' is written as 'UBCMF'. How is 'CHAIR' written in the same code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one: C->D, H->I, A->B, I->J, R->S, giving 'DIBJS'.

questions.question: Which number will replace the question mark: 8, 27, 64, ?, 216
questions.explanation: These are perfect cubes: 2^3,3^3,4^3,5^3,6^3=8,27,64,125,216. Missing term = 125.

questions.question: How many meaningful English words can be formed from the letters 'ACRE' using each letter only once?
questions.explanation: Words like RACE, CARE, ACRE, and ACER can all be formed, so multiple (4) meaningful words are possible.

questions.question: Choose the correct mirror image consideration: If a clock shows 3:15, what will its mirror image show?
questions.explanation: For clock mirror images, the mirror time is found by subtracting the shown time from 12:00(i.e., 11:60); 3:15 mirrors to 9:45.

questions.question: If South-East becomes East, North becomes North-West, and so on in a clockwise sense, what will West become?
questions.explanation: Under a consistent 45-degree anticlockwise relabeling scheme, West becomes South.

questions.question: Statements: All doctors are educated. Some educated people are rich. Conclusion I: Some doctors are rich. Conclusion II: All educated people are doctors. Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Neither conclusion follows definitely because the 'some educated are rich' does not guarantee an overlap with doctors specifically, and Conclusion II reverses the given statement incorrectly.

questions.question: Statements: No pen is a pencil. All pencils are erasers. Conclusion I: No eraser is a pen. Conclusion II: Some erasers are pencils. Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Since all pencils are erasers, some erasers are definitely pencils (Conclusion II). Conclusion I does not necessarily follow from the given statements.

questions.question: How many squares are there in a standard 2x2 grid of unit squares (i.e., made up of 4 small squares)?
questions.explanation: There are 4 small unit squares plus 1 larger square formed by the whole grid, totaling 5 squares.

questions.question: A is the son of B. C is the mother of A. D is the brother of C. How is D related to B?
questions.explanation: Since C is B's spouse (mother of B's son A) and D is C's brother, D is B's brother-in-law.

questions.question: Ramesh walks 6 km East, then turns south and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.explanation: Using the Pythagorean theorem: sqrt(6^2+8^2) = sqrt(36+64) = sqrt(100) = 10 km.

questions.question: If Monday falls on the 1st of a month, what day will fall on the 29th of the same month?
questions.explanation: From the 1st to the 29th is 28 days later. Since 28 is exactly divisible by 7 (4 full weeks), the 29th also falls on a Monday.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word: Water : Thirst :: Food : ?
questions.explanation: Water quenches thirst; similarly, food satisfies hunger.

questions.question: In which year was the first passenger train in India run, between Bombay and Thane?
questions.explanation: India's first passenger train ran between Bombay (Bori Bunder) and Thane on 16th April 1853.

questions.question: Who is credited with laying the foundation of Indian Railways as Governor-General of India?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie is widely credited with laying the foundations of the railway network in India.

questions.question: Which is the headquarters of the Indian Railways' apex body, the Railway Board?
questions.explanation: The Railway Board, which governs Indian Railways, is headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.question: How many zones does Indian Railways currently have?
questions.explanation: Indian Railways currently has 19 zones, including the South Coast Railway zone created with headquarters at Visakhapatnam.

questions.question: Which railway zone has its headquarters at Visakhapatnam?
questions.explanation: South Coast Railway, one of the newer zones, is headquartered at Visakhapatnam.

questions.question: Which is the longest railway platform in the world, located in India?
questions.explanation: Gorakhpur railway station in Uttar Pradesh has the longest railway platform in the world.

questions.question: What is the name of India's first semi-high-speed train, introduced in 2019?
questions.explanation: Vande Bharat Express, launched in 2019, is India's first semi-high-speed indigenous train (Train 18).

questions.question: Which is India's first bullet train corridor currently under construction?
questions.explanation: The Mumbai-Ahmedabad High Speed Rail Corridor is India's first bullet train project, being built with Japanese assistance.

questions.question: What is the gauge width of Indian Railways' 'Broad Gauge' track?
questions.explanation: Broad Gauge, the most widely used gauge in India, has a track width of 1.676 metres.

questions.question: Which is the longest railway route (by distance) operated within India?
questions.explanation: The Vivek Express connecting Dibrugarh (Assam) to Kanyakumari (Tamil Nadu) covers one of the longest railway routes in India.

questions.question: What does IRCTC stand for?
options: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation
Indian Railway Cargo and Transport Company
Indian Rail Coach and Ticketing Centre
Indian Railway Construction and Track Corporation
questions.answer: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation
questions.explanation: IRCTC stands for Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation, which handles catering, tourism, and online ticketing.

questions.question: Which organization manufactures electric locomotives for Indian Railways at Chittaranjan, West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Chittaranjan Locomotive Works (CLW) in West Bengal is a major manufacturer of electric locomotives for Indian Railways.

questions.question: Where is the Rail Coach Factory that manufactures LHB coaches for Indian Railways located?
options: Both Kapurthala and Chennai (Integral Coach Factory)
questions.answer: Both Kapurthala and Chennai (Integral Coach Factory)
questions.explanation: Both the Rail Coach Factory at Kapurthala and the Integral Coach Factory at Chennai manufacture passenger coaches, including LHB coaches, for Indian Railways.

questions.question: Which is the current fastest train in India (as of recent years) in terms of operational speed?
questions.explanation: The Vande Bharat Express is currently among the fastest operational trains in India, capable of speeds up to 180 km/h on some routes.

questions.question: How many fundamental rights are guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution originally guaranteed 7 fundamental rights, but after the 44th Amendment removed the Right to Property, there are now 6 fundamental rights.

questions.question: Who is regarded as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar, as chairman of the Drafting Committee, is regarded as the Father of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: What is the current total number of elected members in the Lok Sabha (House of the People)?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha currently has 543 elected members.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become a member of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 25 years old to be eligible to contest for the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: What is the minimum age required to become a member of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 30 years old to be eligible to contest for the Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India as per constitutional provisions.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Equality?
questions.explanation: Article 14 of the Constitution guarantees the Right to Equality before the law.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution is known as the 'Right to Constitutional Remedies'?
questions.explanation: Article 32 provides the Right to Constitutional Remedies, allowing citizens to move the Supreme Court to enforce fundamental rights.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river flowing within India.

questions.question: Which is the largest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest Indian state by geographical area.

questions.question: Which is the smallest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest Indian state by geographical area.

questions.question: Which is the highest mountain peak in India?
questions.explanation: Kanchenjunga, located in Sikkim, is the highest mountain peak entirely within India.

questions.question: Which desert is located in Rajasthan, India?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert, also called the Great Indian Desert, is located mainly in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which is the southernmost point of mainland India?
questions.explanation: Kanyakumari is the southernmost tip of mainland India, though Indira Point (in the Andamans) is the southernmost point of the country overall.

questions.question: Which two seas/oceans meet at Kanyakumari along with the Indian Ocean?
options: Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal
questions.answer: Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal
questions.explanation: At Kanyakumari, the Arabian Sea, the Bay of Bengal, and the Indian Ocean are said to meet.

questions.question: Which state is known as the 'Spice Garden of India'?
questions.explanation: Kerala is famously known as the 'Spice Garden of India' due to its cultivation of spices like pepper, cardamom, and cloves.

questions.question: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

questions.question: Which is the largest freshwater lake in India?
questions.explanation: Wular Lake in Jammu and Kashmir is the largest freshwater lake in India.

questions.question: Which is the largest saltwater lagoon in India?
questions.explanation: Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest coastal saltwater lagoon in India.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of electric current is the Ampere.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of force is the Newton.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of power is the Watt.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula of common salt?
questions.explanation: Common salt (table salt) has the chemical formula NaCl (sodium chloride).

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the Earth's atmosphere, making it the most abundant gas.

questions.question: Which part of the human body is responsible for pumping blood?
questions.explanation: The heart is the muscular organ responsible for pumping blood throughout the body.

questions.question: Which vitamin is obtained mainly from sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin upon exposure to sunlight.

questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell called?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouse of the cell because they generate most of the cell's energy (ATP).

questions.question: Which scientist proposed the law of gravitation?
questions.explanation: Sir Isaac Newton proposed the universal law of gravitation.

questions.question: Which scientist developed the theory of relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity.

questions.question: What is the pH value of pure water?
questions.explanation: Pure water has a neutral pH value of 7.

questions.question: Which of the following is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal and a good conductor of electricity, widely used in electrical wiring.

questions.question: Which blood group is known as the universal donor?
questions.explanation: O negative blood group is known as the universal donor because it can be given to patients of any blood group.

questions.question: Which blood group is known as the universal recipient?
questions.explanation: AB positive blood group is known as the universal recipient because it can receive blood from any group.

questions.question: Which is the largest organ of the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ of the human body.

questions.question: What is the normal body temperature of a healthy human in Fahrenheit?
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is approximately 98.6 F (37 C).

questions.question: Which gas is commonly known as 'laughing gas'?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide (N2O) is commonly known as 'laughing gas' and is used as an anaesthetic.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for gold is Au, from its Latin name 'aurum'.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for iron?
questions.explanation: The chemical symbol for iron is Fe, from its Latin name 'ferrum'.

questions.question: Which part of the plant conducts photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: The leaf, containing chlorophyll, is the main site of photosynthesis in plants.

questions.question: In which year did India gain independence from British rule?
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule on 15th August 1947.

questions.question: When did the Indian Constitution come into effect?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution came into effect on 26th January 1950, celebrated as Republic Day.

questions.question: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of independent India.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, serving from 1950 to 1962.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Father of the Nation' in India?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi is honoured as the 'Father of the Nation' in India.

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in August 1942.

questions.question: Who founded the Indian National Congress in 1885?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume, a retired British civil servant.

questions.question: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place in which city?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred in Amritsar, Punjab, in April 1919.

questions.question: Who was the Viceroy of India during the partition of Bengal in 1905?
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon, as Viceroy of India, ordered the partition of Bengal in 1905.

questions.question: Who was the last Governor-General and first Prime Minister-appointing Viceroy at the time of India's independence?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten was the last Viceroy of British India and oversaw the transfer of power in 1947.

questions.question: In which sport is the 'Ranji Trophy' associated with in India?
questions.explanation: The Ranji Trophy is a domestic first-class cricket championship played in India.

questions.question: How many players are there in a standard cricket team on the field?
questions.explanation: A standard cricket team fields 11 players at a time.

questions.question: How many players are there in a standard football (soccer) team on the field?
questions.explanation: A standard football team fields 11 players at a time, including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: Which award is the highest civilian award in India?
questions.explanation: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award in India.

questions.question: Which is the highest gallantry award in India?
questions.explanation: The Param Vir Chakra is India's highest military decoration, awarded for the highest degree of valour.

questions.question: Which Indian city is known as the 'Silicon Valley of India'?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is popularly known as the 'Silicon Valley of India' due to its concentration of IT companies.

questions.question: Which is the national bird of India?
questions.explanation: The Indian Peacock (peafowl) is the national bird of India.
